title: hemerocallis citrina var. citrina
intro: Hemerocallis citrina var. citrina, also known as the yellow daylily, is a perennial plant species in the family Hemerocallidaceae, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible parts. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress, improving immune function, and supporting cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
